message:
feat(lab): live runner adapter for differential testing (2a6k9.4.1)

Adds the live-side execution adapter for dual-run differential scenarios:

- LiveRunnerConfig: seed, entropy, profile, scenario/surface identity
- LiveExecutionProfile: CurrentThread (Phase 1 only)
- LiveWitnessCollector: explicit evidence capture via set_outcome(),
  set_cancellation(), set_loser_drain(), record_counter(), etc.
- run_live_adapter(): structured execution with LIVE_RUN_START/COMPLETE
  tracing events, nondeterminism qualifiers, and replay metadata
- LiveRunMetadata, LiveRunResult: structured output with serde support

The adapter is explicit about what it controls (seed, profile, evidence
collection) and what it cannot control (scheduler ordering). It does not
silently turn live execution into another simulated path.

10 new tests covering config construction, witness collection, adapter
execution, cancellation scenarios, metadata serde, and harness integration.
Total: 80 dual_run tests.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

 {"id":"asupersync-2a6k9.3.2","title":"Promote DPOR and schedule exploration outputs into minimized differential scenarios","description":"Make explored schedules first-class differential inputs by preserving the schedule constraints that matter and minimizing them into durable reproducer material.","design":"Extend the DPOR and schedule-exploration path so explored interleavings can be represented as compact scenario/schedule material for the differential framework. The emphasis should be on preserving the semantic schedule constraints that matter, not encoding every incidental detail of one particular execution.

Minimization belongs here too. A good promoted schedule is small enough to reason about, still triggers the same mismatch, and can be fed back into debugging or CI without carrying a huge irrelevant trace burden.","acceptance_criteria":"DPOR or similar schedule exploration can produce replayable differential scenarios or schedule hints, and direct unit/contract coverage proves the promotion path preserves the schedule constraints that actually matter semantically.

The promoted artifacts are minimized enough to be useful in debugging and regression suites, with shrink lineage and retained evidence making clear what corner condition survived minimization.

At least one promoted schedule-derived case is shown end-to-end through the shared replay/report flow, with structured logs, artifact pointers, and reproduction instructions preserving both the original and minimized lineage.","notes":"This task turns schedule exploration from a lab-only investigation tool into a source of durable cross-runtime test inputs.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","estimated_minutes":180,"created_at":"2026-03-18T19:55:20.347589283Z","created_by":"TurquoiseHawk","updated_at":"2026-03-18T21:06:57.687757904Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["differential","dpor","replay","schedules","verification"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"asupersync-2a6k9.3.2","depends_on_id":"asupersync-2a6k9.2.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-18T20:04:31.674448622Z","created_by":"TurquoiseHawk","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"asupersync-2a6k9.3.2","depends_on_id":"asupersync-2a6k9.2.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-18T20:04:34.677039510Z","created_by":"TurquoiseHawk","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"asupersync-2a6k9.3.2","depends_on_id":"asupersync-2a6k9.3","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-18T19:55:20.347589283Z","created_by":"TurquoiseHawk","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"asupersync-2a6k9.3.3","title":"Define divergence corpus registry, shrinker rules, and retention metadata","description":"Create the long-lived storage and lifecycle rules for mismatch-causing seeds so discoveries survive debugging and become durable regression coverage when appropriate.","design":"Define how mismatch-causing scenarios are named, stored, shrunk, and retained over time. This includes metadata such as originating surface, seed lineage, shrink status, first-seen context, and whether the case is now a permanent regression or still under investigation.

The retention rules should support both debugging and long-term project hygiene. The corpus should grow by admitting high-value seeds, not by hoarding opaque one-offs forever. Shrinking should preserve semantic mismatch class, not just reduce byte size or step count mechanically.","acceptance_criteria":"There is a documented registry format and lifecycle for divergence-causing seeds and scenarios.

Registry metadata records at least surface/profile context, divergence class, first-seen lineage, minimization lineage, artifact pointers, and regression-promotion state.

The registry rules are exercised by direct validation tests and by at least one end-to-end promotion path so future contributors can add new mismatch artifacts without improvising storage conventions or losing provenance needed for debugging, reporting, or replay.","notes":"A corpus without lifecycle rules becomes a junk drawer. This task keeps the artifact story sustainable.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","estimated_minutes":150,"created_at":"2026-03-18T19:55:26.265634606Z","created_by":"TurquoiseHawk","updated_at":"2026-03-18T20:51:47.399409203Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["corpus","differential","regression","shrink","verification"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"asupersync-2a6k9.3.3","depends_on_id":"asupersync-2a6k9.1.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-18T20:04:43.539868544Z","created_by":"TurquoiseHawk","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"asupersync-2a6k9.3.3","depends_on_id":"asupersync-2a6k9.3","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-18T19:55:26.265634606Z","created_by":"TurquoiseHawk","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"asupersync-2a6k9.3.3","depends_on_id":"asupersync-2a6k9.3.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-18T20:04:37.607638857Z","created_by":"TurquoiseHawk","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"asupersync-2a6k9.3.3","depends_on_id":"asupersync-2a6k9.3.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-18T20:04:40.554822795Z","created_by":"TurquoiseHawk","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"asupersync-2a6k9.4","title":"Capture live-runtime semantic evidence and normalize it for comparison","description":"Build the live-side adapter, instrumentation, and normalization pipeline needed to compare real executions against lab expectations without depending on fragile raw event-order equality, and verify that translation layer directly with deterministic fixtures and mismatch diagnostics.","design":"This phase makes live execution observable enough to compare meaningfully. The requirement is not perfect raw trace identity; it is sufficient semantic evidence to answer the comparator questions defined in the contract phase.

Live-side collection should prefer stable semantic markers over brittle internal details. The runtime adapter should aim for execution paths that reduce gratuitous scheduler noise, such as current-thread setups where appropriate, while still exercising the actual live code paths under test.

Normalization is a first-class design concern here. The output should collapse lab and live evidence into the same semantic record type so the differential engine is comparing comparable facts instead of raw heterogeneous traces.","acceptance_criteria":"There is a live-runtime adapter that can execute differential scenarios and emit semantic evidence suitable for comparison.

Normalization rules exist for the main observables under test and avoid depending on unstable raw ordering where ordering is not part of the contract.

The live capture and normalization path has dedicated unit/fixture coverage, representative golden records, and field-level mismatch diagnostics rather than being tested only transitively through later pilots.","notes":"This phase should reuse test setup and runtime-builder patterns already present in `tests/common/mod.rs` and related runtime tests. It is intentionally separate from the comparator phase because evidence collection and mismatch judgment are different responsibilities.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"feature","estimated_minutes":720,"created_at":"2026-03-18T19:50:05.880143236Z","created_by":"TurquoiseHawk","updated_at":"2026-03-18T21:01:52.000549348Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["differential","epic","evidence","live","trace","verification"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"asupersync-2a6k9.4","depends_on_id":"asupersync-2a6k9","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-18T19:50:05.880143236Z","created_by":"TurquoiseHawk","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"asupersync-2a6k9.4","depends_on_id":"asupersync-2a6k9.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-18T21:01:52.000491300Z","created_by":"TurquoiseHawk","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"asupersync-2a6k9.4","depends_on_id":"asupersync-2a6k9.1.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-18T20:03:11.051481361Z","created_by":"TurquoiseHawk","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"asupersync-2a6k9.4","depends_on_id":"asupersync-2a6k9.2.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-18T20:03:14.016051971Z","created_by":"TurquoiseHawk","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
-{"id":"asupersync-2a6k9.4.1","title":"Build current-thread live runner adapter for differential scenarios","description":"Create the live execution adapter that runs shared scenarios through real runtime code paths while minimizing gratuitous scheduling noise and preserving reproducibility.","design":"Build the live-side execution adapter that can take a shared scenario and drive the actual runtime paths under test with as little incidental nondeterminism as practical. Prefer current-thread or otherwise constrained runtime setups where that reduces scheduler noise without changing the semantics the surface is supposed to exercise.

The adapter should be explicit about what it controls and what it cannot control. It must not quietly turn live execution into another simulated path. The whole point is to run the real code paths while still making comparison practical.","acceptance_criteria":"A live-run adapter exists and can execute shared differential scenarios through real runtime code paths using a documented execution model rather than ad hoc per-suite setup.

Adapter bootstrapping, run lifecycle, and teardown behavior have direct unit or contract coverage plus at least one smoke-level e2e path using the same live-run surface the later pilots and scripts will use.

The adapter emits structured run metadata rich enough for later reports and replay guidance, including scenario identity, profile, seed lineage, runtime mode, and any known nondeterminism qualifiers.","notes":"This task is about controlling noise, not about hiding the fact that the live path is live.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","estimated_minutes":180,"created_at":"2026-03-18T19:56:57.416189957Z","created_by":"TurquoiseHawk","updated_at":"2026-03-18T21:07:14.875158738Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["differential","live","runner","verification"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"asupersync-2a6k9.4.1","depends_on_id":"asupersync-2a6k9.1.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-18T20:04:46.467178655Z","created_by":"TurquoiseHawk","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"asupersync-2a6k9.4.1","depends_on_id":"asupersync-2a6k9.1.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-18T20:04:49.469850852Z","created_by":"TurquoiseHawk","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"asupersync-2a6k9.4.1","depends_on_id":"asupersync-2a6k9.2.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-18T20:04:52.401530918Z","created_by":"TurquoiseHawk","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"asupersync-2a6k9.4.1","depends_on_id":"asupersync-2a6k9.4","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-18T19:56:57.416189957Z","created_by":"TurquoiseHawk","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}

Prefer current-thread or otherwise constrained runtime setups where that reduces scheduler noise without changing the semantics the surface is supposed to exercise.

The adapter should be explicit about what it controls and what it cannot control. It must not quietly turn live execution into another simulated path. The whole point is to run the real code paths while still making comparison practical.","acceptance_criteria":"A live-run adapter exists and can execute shared differential scenarios through real runtime code paths using a documented execution model rather than ad hoc per-suite setup.

Adapter bootstrapping, run lifecycle, and teardown behavior have direct unit or contract coverage plus at least one smoke-level e2e path using the same live-run surface the later pilots and scripts will use.

The adapter emits structured run metadata rich enough for later reports and replay guidance, including scenario identity, profile, seed lineage, runtime mode, and any known nondeterminism qualifiers.","notes":"This task is about controlling noise, not about hiding the fact that the live path is live.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","estimated_minutes":180,"created_at":"2026-03-18T19:56:57.416189957Z","created_by":"TurquoiseHawk","updated_at":"2026-03-19T02:23:26.562404216Z","closed_at":"2026-03-19T02:23:26.562379841Z","close_reason":"Implemented LiveRunnerAdapter: LiveRunnerConfig, LiveExecutionProfile, LiveWitnessCollector, LiveRunMetadata, LiveRunResult, run_live_adapter(). 10 new tests (total 80). Current-thread adapter with explicit witness collection, structured metadata logging, nondeterminism qualifiers, and full integration with DualRunHarness.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["differential","live","runner","verification"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"asupersync-2a6k9.4.1","depends_on_id":"asupersync-2a6k9.1.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-18T20:04:46.467178655Z","created_by":"TurquoiseHawk","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"asupersync-2a6k9.4.1","depends_on_id":"asupersync-2a6k9.1.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-18T20:04:49.469850852Z","created_by":"TurquoiseHawk","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"asupersync-2a6k9.4.1","depends_on_id":"asupersync-2a6k9.2.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-18T20:04:52.401530918Z","created_by":"TurquoiseHawk","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"asupersync-2a6k9.4.1","depends_on_id":"asupersync-2a6k9.4","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-18T19:56:57.416189957Z","created_by":"TurquoiseHawk","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"asupersync-2a6k9.4.2","title":"Add live-side semantic capture hooks for comparison-relevant events","description":"Expose the semantic facts from live executions that the comparator needs, focusing on stable hooks rather than brittle raw traces or text logs.","design":"Instrument the live runtime just enough to recover the semantic facts defined by the comparator contract. That means capturing events and counters relevant to outcomes, cancellation lifecycle, loser drain, region quiescence, obligation settlement, and any surface-specific invariants chosen for early pilots.

Instrumentation should prefer stable semantic hooks over brittle log scraping or raw trace deltas. If a surface cannot be observed well enough yet, that should become a first-class limitation rather than an excuse to compare on shaky proxies.","acceptance_criteria":"The live execution path exposes the semantic evidence required by the normalized comparator schema through stable semantic hooks rather than fragile textual logs or incidental implementation details.

Missing observability is explicit and machine-readable rather than silently approximated, and capture-field behavior is covered by direct unit or fixture checks instead of being trusted only transitively.

The emitted evidence payloads and logs are structured enough that later normalization, reports, and crashpacks can trust the field names, missingness semantics, and provenance of each captured fact.","notes":"Better instrumentation beats more tolerant comparison logic. This task should bias toward improving evidence quality.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","estimated_minutes":180,"created_at":"2026-03-18T19:57:03.391447578Z","created_by":"TurquoiseHawk","updated_at":"2026-03-18T21:07:32.082303978Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["differential","instrumentation","live","verification"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"asupersync-2a6k9.4.2","depends_on_id":"asupersync-2a6k9.1.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-18T20:04:58.499190711Z","created_by":"TurquoiseHawk","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"asupersync-2a6k9.4.2","depends_on_id":"asupersync-2a6k9.4","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-18T19:57:03.391447578Z","created_by":"TurquoiseHawk","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"asupersync-2a6k9.4.2","depends_on_id":"asupersync-2a6k9.4.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-18T20:04:55.386840494Z","created_by":"TurquoiseHawk","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"asupersync-2a6k9.4.3","title":"Normalize lab and live evidence into shared comparator records","description":"Build the actual normalization path that converts heterogeneous lab/live evidence into the same semantic record type for comparison.","design":"Implement the mapping that takes lab evidence and live evidence and collapses both into the canonical normalized record defined earlier. This includes canonical ordering for data that is semantically unordered, consistent naming for obligations and regions, and explicit elision of incidental detail.

Where the mapping is lossy, the loss must be intentional and justified. The project should be able to explain exactly why omitted detail is not needed to judge semantic parity for the chosen surface.","acceptance_criteria":"There is a concrete normalization path from both execution environments into the shared comparator record, and unit or fixture coverage exercises representative positive and negative mappings directly.

Canonical example mappings and golden record shapes are pinned, and field-level diff output plus structured diagnostics make false-positive and false-negative risk reviewable when normalization behavior changes.

At least one reusable downstream consumer beyond the local normalization tests uses the same normalized shape end-to-end, so later runner and reporting flows are not quietly relying on a different record contract.","notes":"This task is where semantic comparison becomes real instead of aspirational.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","estimated_minutes":180,"created_at":"2026-03-18T19:57:09.263052027Z","created_by":"TurquoiseHawk","updated_at":"2026-03-18T21:07:49.279820374Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["differential","normalization","observability","verification"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"asupersync-2a6k9.4.3","depends_on_id":"asupersync-2a6k9.1.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-18T20:05:04.471910998Z","created_by":"TurquoiseHawk","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"asupersync-2a6k9.4.3","depends_on_id":"asupersync-2a6k9.4","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-18T19:57:09.263052027Z","created_by":"TurquoiseHawk","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"asupersync-2a6k9.4.3","depends_on_id":"asupersync-2a6k9.4.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-18T20:05:01.449827579Z","created_by":"TurquoiseHawk","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"asupersync-2a6k9.4.4","title":"Define time and scheduler-noise normalization policy for live re-execution","description":"Specify how virtual time, wall clock, and scheduler noise should be treated when comparing lab and live results so the framework stays semantically sharp without becoming brittle.","design":"Define how the framework reconciles virtual time, wall clock, and scheduler noise during live re-execution. Some surfaces will require ordering or timing windows to be normalized; others must reject timing-sensitive comparison entirely until the effect boundary is better controlled.

This task should set the explicit policy for when time-based facts are comparable, when they are only advisory, and when they are out of scope. It should also say how reruns interact with timing noise so the comparator does not accidentally ratchet itself into useless tolerance.","acceptance_criteria":"There is a written normalization policy for time-related and scheduler-noise-related evidence.

The policy makes it clear which time facts are part of semantic comparison and which are not.

The policy also defines how suppression, qualification, or explanation of scheduler noise appears in reports and verdict semantics so later CI and operator flows do not invent their own tolerance language.","notes":"This task exists because timing ambiguity is one of the fastest ways to destroy confidence in differential testing.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","estimated_minutes":150,"created_at":"2026-03-18T19:57:15.245634382Z","created_by":"TurquoiseHawk","updated_at":"2026-03-18T20:47:36.097031465Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["differential","normalization","time","verification"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"asupersync-2a6k9.4.4","depends_on_id":"asupersync-2a6k9.1.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-18T20:05:07.420718160Z","created_by":"TurquoiseHawk","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"asupersync-2a6k9.4.4","depends_on_id":"asupersync-2a6k9.1.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-18T20:05:10.395240991Z","created_by":"TurquoiseHawk","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"asupersync-2a6k9.4.4","depends_on_id":"asupersync-2a6k9.4","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-18T19:57:15.245634382Z","created_by":"TurquoiseHawk","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"asupersync-2a6k9.4.4","depends_on_id":"asupersync-2a6k9.4.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-18T20:05:13.352500745Z","created_by":"TurquoiseHawk","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
